title: Prompt Lifecycle, Monitoring, and Production Capstone · Mastery Check
summary: Ship and maintain a cross-provider prompt system with regression evidence. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a production prompt portfolio and release gate. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Store owners, versions, model assumptions, templates, and evaluation links in a prompt registry.
Block prompt releases on material deterministic, rubric, safety, or human-review regressions.
Monitor outcome, refusal, tool, latency, cost, and drift signals without logging sensitive content unnecessarily.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Prompt Registry evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Prompt Registry assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Store owners, versions, model assumptions, templates, and evaluation links in a prompt registry. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt production capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Prompt Registry, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a production prompt portfolio and release gate.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Prompt text can be edited in production without traceability. This fails the Prompt Registry contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Anthropic Prompting Best Practices (Current Claude model guidance) — Controls clear instructions, examples, structure, reasoning, tools, and model-specific migration cautions.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Store owners, versions, model assumptions, templates, and evaluation links in a prompt registry.

hints: Name the Prompt Registry precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: prompt production capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Prompt text can be edited in production without traceability.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Portfolio Defense applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend prompt design choices with evidence across Claude, Codex, changed cases, and limitations.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Prompt Registry to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Prompt Registry.

checks.description: The decision applies Regression Gates to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Regression Gates.

checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Online Monitoring.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using defend and prompt plus specific case evidence.
